req.title: Update patient (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) successful demographic update with version history preserved, (2) AuditEvent emission for every operation, and (3) 409 on version conflict. Four tests are designed: a positive EP/STC test confirming update + history + audit; a negative NEG test for version conflict (409); a boundary BVA test for updating with exactly the current versionId (off-by-one boundary); and a DT test covering the decision matrix of role × version correctness × resource existence. Together they achieve full coverage of the stated outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_update_patient_demographics_preserves_history_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid Bearer token
A Patient resource with id=<test_patient_id> exists at version 1
actions: PUT /Patient/<test_patient_id> with If-Match header set to current versionId and updated family name
GET /Patient/<test_patient_id>/_history to verify both versions exist
expected: Response body versionId is incremented by 1
AuditEvent list contains at least one record with action='U' referencing the patient id
trace: R-HLT-078: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_update_patient_demographics_preserves_history_and_emits_audit(
    clinician_session,   # <fixture> returns requests.Session with auth header
    base_url,            # <fixture> returns str e.g. 'http://fhir-lite'
    existing_patient,    # <fixture> creates patient, yields {id, versionId}
):
    pid = existing_patient["id"]
    v1  = existing_patient["versionId"]

    payload = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(pid, v1, family="UpdatedFamily")
    resp = clinician_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{v1}"'},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    v2 = body["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert int(v2) == int(v1) + 1

    hist = clinician_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}/_history")
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    entries = hist.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2

    audit = clinician_session.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{pid}", "action": "U"},
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    audit_entries = audit.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in audit_entries]
    assert "U" in actions

tests.name: test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid Bearer token
A Patient resource with id=<test_patient_id> exists at version 3
Caller intentionally supplies a stale versionId (version 1)
actions: PUT /Patient/<test_patient_id> with If-Match header set to stale versionId=1
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code='conflict'
No new version is created in history
trace: R-HLT-078: error pathway - 409 on version conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409(
    clinician_session,
    base_url,
    existing_patient_at_version_3,  # <fixture> patient at versionId=3
):
    pid         = existing_patient_at_version_3["id"]
    stale_vid   = "1"

    payload = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(pid, stale_vid, family="StaleUpdate")
    resp = clinician_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{stale_vid}"'},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    codes = [i.get("code") for i in issues]
    assert "conflict" in codes

    hist = clinician_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}/_history")
    versions = [e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in hist.json().get("entry", [])]
    assert "4" not in versions

tests.name: test_update_patient_with_exact_current_versionid_succeeds
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid Bearer token
A Patient resource exists with a known current versionId N
Test supplies If-Match exactly equal to N (not N-1, not N+1)
actions: GET /Patient/<id> to read exact current versionId N
PUT /Patient/<id> with If-Match: W/"N" (no off-by-one deviation)
trace: R-HLT-078: BVA on versionId boundary - exact match vs conflict boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_update_patient_with_exact_current_versionid_succeeds(
    clinician_session,
    base_url,
    existing_patient,
):
    pid = existing_patient["id"]

    get_resp = clinician_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    current_vid = get_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    expected_next = str(int(current_vid) + 1)

    payload = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(pid, current_vid, family="BoundaryFamily")
    put_resp = clinician_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{current_vid}"'},
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    new_vid = put_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert new_vid == expected_next

    hist = clinician_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}/_history")
    versions = [
        e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"]
        for e in hist.json().get("entry", [])
    ]
    assert expected_next in versions
    assert current_vid in versions

tests.name: test_update_patient_decision_table_role_version_existence
preconditions: Two sessions available: clinician_session (role:clinician) and guest_session (role:none)
A Patient resource exists with a known id and current versionId
A non-existent patient id is available (uuid not in store)
actions: Attempt PUT as guest (no role) with correct versionId - expect 403
Attempt PUT as clinician with correct versionId on non-existent id - expect 404
Confirm clinician with correct versionId on existing id returns 200 (control row)
expected: Clinician PUT on missing resource returns HTTP 404
Clinician PUT on existing resource returns HTTP 200
trace: R-HLT-078: DT covers role × existence × version correctness decision matrix
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_update_patient_decision_table_role_version_existence(
    clinician_session,
    guest_session,       # <fixture> unauthenticated / no-role session
    base_url,
    existing_patient,
):
    pid     = existing_patient["id"]
    vid     = existing_patient["versionId"]
    missing = str(uuid.uuid4())

    # Row 1: wrong role, correct version, existing resource -> 403
    payload_existing = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(pid, vid, family="GuestAttempt")
    r_forbidden = guest_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload_existing,
        headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"'},
    )
    assert r_forbidden.status_code == 403

    # Row 2: correct role, correct version, non-existent resource -> 404
    payload_missing = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(missing, "1", family="GhostPatient")
    r_not_found = clinician_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{missing}",
        json=payload_missing,
        headers={"If-Match": 'W/"1"'},
    )
    assert r_not_found.status_code == 404

    # Row 3 (control): correct role, correct version, existing resource -> 200
    payload_ok = <helper_fn>.patient_payload(pid, vid, family="DTableFamily")
    r_ok = clinician_session.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload_ok,
        headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"'},
    )
    assert r_ok.status_code == 200
